data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0829 GMT - Philippine GDP growth should be steady this year, as interest-rate cuts and low inflation could help offset the drag from weaker exports and tighter fiscal policy, says Capital Economics. In 1Q, growth accelerated slightly on quarter. Export growth accelerated, but this is "likely to go into reverse soon" with a subdued outlook for global growth and amid risks from higher tariffs, says senior Asia economist Gareth Leather in a note. Unlike other countries in the region, the Philippines isn't especially well-placed to benefit from a drop in Chinese exports to the U.S., he says. However, consumption should remain strong, thanks to lower inflation and rate cuts, he adds. Capital Economics forecasts the Philippine economy to expand 6.0% this year. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0825 GMT - The Norwegian krone remains on the back foot after the Norges Bank held its key interest rate at 4.5%, as widely expected, and reiterated that it expects to start cutting rates later this year. "There is uncertainty about future economic developments, but the Committee's current assessment of the outlook implies that the policy rate will most likely be reduced in the course of 2025," the central bank Deputy Governor Pal Longva said. Inflation has fallen markedly but remains above target, he said. If the policy rate is lowered prematurely, prices may continue to rise rapidly. The euro rises 0.1% to 11.7141 krone, compared to 11.7214 before the decision. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0821 GMT - Sterling could rally if the Bank of England reiterates a gradual approach to interest-rate cuts at Thursday's meeting, ING's Chris Turner says in a note. The BOE is expected to cut rates by 25 basis points in a decision at 1102 GMT but some market participants expect it to drop its "gradual and careful" reference about future cuts. "Given that the market is now pricing four 25 basis-point rate cuts this year and we expect three (May, August, November), if BOE easing remains gradual and careful, sterling could rally," Turner says. In this case the euro could fall to 0.8435-0.8440 pounds, he says. the euro last trades down 0.1% at 0.8501 pounds. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0819 GMT - The Philippines' lower-than-expected 1Q GDP growth supports ING's call for the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas to cut rates by 75 bps this year. The downside surprise was primarily driven by weaker net exports and business-process outsourcing activity, says Deepali Bhargava, regional head of research for Asia-Pacific in a note. "Looking ahead, the impact of the global slowdown should become more evident in the Philippines' growth numbers," she writes. However, continued support from government spending and monetary policy easing is likely to offset the drag, she says. Given the latest print and expected slowdown in net export growth, ING lowers its 2025 GDP growth forecast for the Philippines to 5.6%.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0809 GMT - Yields on U.K. 2-year government bonds edge higher ahead of a Bank of England interest rate decision at 1102 GMT, where a 25 basis points interest rate cut is widely expected and focus will center on any signals on when rates could fall again. Investors also anticipate that a possible trade deal between the U.S. and the U.K. could be announced later after President Donald Trump flagged that a major trade deal would be forthcoming. This could help ease concerns about the economic impact of tariffs on the U.K. economy. Two-year gilt yields rise 1 basis point to 3.806%, Tradeweb data show. Ten-year gilt yields are little changed at 4.448%. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0754 GMT - Gold futures fall on a stronger U.S. dollar, optimism for a U.S.-U.K. trade deal and some lingering disappointment after the Federal Reserve left interest rates unchanged. Futures are down 1.2% at $3,351.50 a troy ounce. The Fed indicated it wasn't in a rush to cut rates, despite Chair Jerome Powell warning that if tariffs are sustained, inflation will rise, growth will slow and unemployment will increase, ANZ Research analysts say in a note. Powell's comments triggered a rally in the dollar, keeping downward pressure on bullion, ANZ says. A stronger U.S. dollar improves its safe-haven aspects, competing with the yellow metal. Expectations for a U.S.-U.K. trade deal also added to downside pressure on gold, as the market shifts into a risk-on mood, further sapping gold's safe-haven appeal. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

0751 GMT - Malaysia's central bank kept its benchmark rate at 3.0% but had a more downbeat economic outlook, likely opening the door to rate cuts later this year, says Capital Economics' senior Asia economist Gareth Leather in a note. Bank Negara Malaysia appears increasingly concerned about slowing growth and the fallout from trade tensions, suggesting a shift toward a more accommodative stance, he notes. While Trump's 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs offers temporary relief, Malaysia's semiconductor exports may remain vulnerable to U.S. actions once its national-security probes conclude, he says. Export earnings are also pressured by weaker commodity prices, he adds. With inflation easing and GDP growth weakening, Capital Economics expects BNM to cut rates by 50 bps to 2.50% by year-end, contrary to market consensus for a hold. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0751 GMT - The Swedish krona stays weaker against the euro after Sweden's central bank left its benchmark interest rate unchanged at 2.25%, as expected by markets. While uncertainty remains elevated, it sees the prospect of inflation being lower than previously anticipated, suggesting a "slight easing" of monetary policy going forward. However, the Riksbank said it was "wise to await further information to obtain a clearer picture of the outlook for economic activity and inflation." The euro rises 0.1% to 10.9406 krona, compared to 10.9450 just before the decision. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0748 GMT - Stock markets rise on increased optimism about easing trade tensions after U.S. President Donald Trump said that he would announce a major trade deal later. People familiar with the negotiations said Trump would announce a framework of a trade deal with the U.K. "The news boosted sentiment across global equity markets, lifting Nasdaq and S&P 500 futures, while also sending FTSE futures and sterling higher in Asian trading," IG analysts say in a note. The pan-European index Stoxx Europe 600 climbs 0.2% to 534.95 while the U.K. FTSE 100 stocks index rises 0.1% to 8,564.7 points and U.S. stock futures point to a higher opening on Wall Street. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0746 GMT - The People's Bank of China will likely to stay on an easing path for the rest of 2025 to counter downward pressure on the economy, BNP Paribas analysts write in a note. The brokerage expects a 20bp cuts in the policy rate in 2H and see an additional 50bp cut in the required reserve ratio for the rest of this year, they say. However, the biggest policy driver would be U.S. tariffs, which remain highly uncertain, they add. The brokerage thinks more forceful stimulus will be introduced if current U.S. tariffs persist longer than its base-case assumption that tariffs will be cut to 50% later this quarter, they say.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0742 GMT - UBS Global Wealth Management affirms its expectation for 100 basis points of interest-rate cuts by the Federal Reserve starting in September, says CIO Mark Haefele in a note. Haefele nevertheless acknowledges that "a broad range of outcomes is possible depending on how trade policy evolves." The Fed left its policy target range unchanged at 4.25%-4.50% on Wednesday and Chair Jerome Powell emphasized the central bank was in no rush to consider rate cuts yet. Powell warned that tariffs risked both higher unemployment and higher inflation. Money markets are pricing around a 76% chance of a 25 basis-point rate cut as early as July and are fully pricing one for September, LSEG data show. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0721 GMT - The Swedish krona and Norwegian krone fall as investors trade with caution ahead of decisions from the two countries' central banks, where both are expected to keep interest rates on hold. Sweden's Riksbank is likely to remain in "wait and see mode" for the foreseeable future, Pepperstone strategist Michael Brown says in a note. The Norges Bank could reiterate its prior view that rates will likely be cut at some point this year, though it "probably won't pull the trigger" until the second half of 2025, he says. The euro rises 0.1% to 10.9393 Swedish krona and 0.3% to 11.7298 Norwegian krone. The Riksbank decision is at 0730 GMT and the Norges Bank's decision is at 0800 GMT. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Report from Annual General Meeting in Vivesto AB
data.description:
At the Annual General Meeting in Vivesto AB (“Vivesto” or the “company”) held on 8 May 2025, the following resolutions were passed. All resolutions were in accordance with the proposals, described in detail in the general meeting documents which are available at the company’s website, www.vivesto.com.

Adoption of the income statement and balance sheet

It was resolved to adopt the income statement and balance sheet for the financial year 2024.

Resolution on dispositions in respect of the company’s result

It was resolved that no dividend is paid and that available profits are carried forward.

Resolution on discharge from liability

It was resolved to discharge all members of the Board of Directors and the Chief Executive Officer from liability for the financial year 2024.

Election of Board of Directors and auditor

It was resolved that the number of members of the Board of Directors shall be four, without deputies. It was resolved on re-election of Hege Hellström, Pål Ryfors, Roger Tell and Peter Zonabend as members of the Board of Directors for the period until the end of the next Annual General Meeting.

It was resolved to re-elect Peter Zonabend as Chairman of the Board.

It was resolved that one registered accounting firm be elected as auditor, without deputies. It was resolved to re-elect the registered accounting firm Grant Thornton Sweden AB as the company’s auditor for the period until the end of the next Annual General Meeting. The Authorized Public Accountant Therese Utengen will be appointed auditor in charge.

Resolution on fees to the members of the Board of Directors and the auditor

It was resolved that the fees to the members of the Board of Directors shall be distributed as follows:

  • SEK 500,000 to the Chairman of the Board and SEK 250,000 to each of the other Board members elected by the Annual General Meeting who are not employed by the company, and
  • SEK 50,000 to the Chairman of the Audit Committee and SEK 25,000 to each of the other members of the Audit Committee, and SEK 50,000 to the Chairman of the Remuneration Committee and SEK 25,000 to each of the other members of the Remuneration Committee.

It was resolved that the auditor’s fees shall be paid as per approved invoice.

Resolution on approval of remuneration report

It was resolved to approve the Board of Directors’ report regarding remuneration pursuant to Chapter 8, Section 53 a of the Swedish Companies Act.

Resolution on instructions for the Nomination Committee

It was resolved to adopt new principles for appointing a Nomination Committee and instructions for the Nomination Committee’s work.

Resolution on authorization for the Board of Directors to resolve upon issues of shares, warrants and/or convertible instruments

It was resolved to authorize the Board of Directors to, on one or several occasions during the period up until the next Annual General Meeting and and within the limits of the Articles of Association in force at the time, resolve on issue of shares, warrants and/or convertible instruments, with or without deviation from the shareholders’ preferential rights. Payment may, in addition to cash payment, be made in kind or by set-off, or otherwise with conditions.

The purpose of the authorization and the reasons for any deviation from the shareholders’ preferential rights is to increase the company’s financial flexibility and the Board’s room for maneuver to raise capital in a time- and cost-effective manner and/or complement the shareholder base with new shareholders of strategic importance for the company, and enable the company to, fully or partially, finance any company acquisitions or acquisitions of businesses by issuing financial instruments as payment in connection with acquisitions, or to raise capital for such acquisitions. Issues made with deviation from the shareholders’ preferential rights shall take place at a subscription price in line with market conditions, including any discount in line with market conditions where applicable. If the Board of Directors deems it appropriate in order to enable the delivery of shares in connection with an issue as described above, the issue may be done at a subscription price equal to the quota value of the share.

data.title: Krakatoa Resources to Attend 121 Mining Investment Conference in London
data.description:
Perth, Western Australia--(Newsfile Corp. - May 8, 2025) - Krakatoa Resources is pleased to announce the company is participating in the upcoming 121 Mining Investment Conference in London. Mark Major, CEO of Krakatoa Resources will be presenting about the Company's recent and future planned activities.

121 Mining Investment London will be hosting over 110 mining companies and more than 650 sophisticated investors for two days of pre-arranged, targeted 1-2-1 meetings.

Alongside the curated schedule of pre-booked meetings matching investors with appropriate projects, the conference programme will provide expert commentary and the latest market intelligence on key industry developments.

This year’s event is being held on May 12-13.

Any investors who would like to attend 121 Mining Investment London can register for a free pass here.

About 121 Mining Investment

The 121 Mining Investment global event series connects portfolio managers and analysts from institutional funds, private equity groups and family offices with mining company management teams for 1-2-1, private in-person meetings.

121 Mining Investment has an ever-expanding global portfolio, currently covering London, New York, Cape Town, Dubai and Singapore, as well as online editions throughout the year.

About Krakatoa Resources

Krakatoa Resources is advancing the high-grade antimony and gold Zopkhito Project in Georgia. Krakatoa is commencing exploration work to turn the high-grade foreign resource in to a JORC Resource, with modelling supporting the continuation of the Sb-Au system to depths well below previous exploration and potential for parallel mineralisation veins untouched by exploration. We look forward to meeting with key stakeholders and investors.

For additional information, please contact:

Krakatoa Resources

Mark Major

CEO

+61 447 954 112

mark.major@ktaresources.com

www.ktaresources.com

data.title: The General Meeting in Medistim ASA decided a dividend of NOK 6.00 per share
data.description:
Oslo, the 8th of May 2025. The votes from the General Meeting today inMedistimASA, has resolved to distribute a dividend of NOK 6.00 per share based on theCompany's net profit for the 2024 financial year. The total dividend willamountto NOK 109.9 million, which corresponds to 106% of the net profit for 2024.Thedividend will be payable to the shareholders of the Company as of the 8th ofMay2025. The shares will be traded exclusive of the right to dividends from andincluding the 9th of May 2025 (ex-date). The dividend will be distributed the19th of May 2025.

https://newsweb.oslobors.no/message/645719

data.title: Japan Private Sector Expands in April On Services: PMI Report
data.description:
Japan's private sector expanded in April, but largely on the strength in the services sector as factories lagged, reported S&P Global on Wednesday.

The Japan composite purchasing managers index (PMI), a combination of the nation's manufacturing and services sectors, logged at 51.2 in April, up from 48.9 in March, and striking above the 50-mark that separates growth from contraction, reported S&P Global, citing its monthly survey.

However, the Japan manufacturing PMI only rose modestly to 48.7 in April from 48.4 in March, registering still in the red zone, and thus signaling "a deterioration in business conditions for the tenth month in a row, albeit one that was modest overall," advised S&P Global.

In April, manufacturers reported "the steepest drop in new export work for six months," said S&P Global.

In contrast, the Japan services PMI rose to 52.4 in April from 50.0 in March "to indicate a fresh increase in service sector activity across Japan. explained S&P Global.

In April, business growth "was predominantly driven

by the service sector, which moved back into expansion territory in April, as factory output continued to decline," said S&P Global.

The outlook for Japan's private-sector, especially manufacturing, has been made less certain following recent tariffs on Japan's exports implemented by the Trump Administration in Washington.

In early May, the Bank of Japan lowered its forecast for growth of the nation's gross domestic product (GDP) in fiscal 2025 (started April 1) to 0.5% from 1.1%, citing the probable impact of Trump tariffs.

The Japan composite PMI was compiled by S&P Global from surveys sent to 400 manufacturers and also 400 service-sector companies, from April 9 through April 25.

data.title: UK's Centrica tumbles after AGM update; Energy, Storage+ units flag weaker outlook
data.description:
** British energy supplier Centrica down as much as 7.9% at 146.4p, stock among top loser on FTSE 100 CURRENCYCOM:UK100 index

** Co maintains annual outlook overall and intention to increase dividends, but flags pressures on some of its businesses

** Says unit Centrica Energy is expected to deliver full-year profit near the bottom of its medium-term adj operating profit range, with profitability weighted towards H2

** Losses at Energy Storage+ unit now expected at higher end of forecast range

** Says British Gas Residential Energy was hit by warmer than normal weather in Q2 but profit for 2025 is expected to be within medium-term targets

** Adds it currently does not anticipate any material impact on its business or financial results from trade tariffs

** YTD CNA up about 9.5%

($1 = 0.7532 pounds)

data.title: Emirati Logistics Major Aramex Sees First-quarter Profit Slump From Nearshoring
data.description:
Aramex's (DFM:ARMX) attributable profit in the first quarter of 2025 plummeted 63% as the nearshoring trend diminished international express sales while inflationary pressure and direct costs rose year over year, according to a late Wednesday filing.

Profit attributable to equity holders of the parent for the three months ended March 31 amounted to 17.1 million Emirati dirhams, down from 46.6 million dirhams earlier. EPS moved to 0.012 dirham from 0.032 dirham.

The Dubai-listed logistics and transportation services company's gross profit slipped to 364.7 million dirhams from 395.4 million dirhams, while revenue rose slightly to 1.56 billion dirhams from 1.54 billion dirhams a year ago.

Divisionally, revenue from domestic express climbed 13% as businesses continued to streamline supply chains by positioning inventory closer to key markets. The nearshoring impact also benefited warehousing but dragged international express revenues down 13%.

At the same time, growth in e-commerce and energy propelled freight forwarding sales up 9%, while logistics revenues zoomed up 21% on the back of higher warehouse utilization, new client contracts, and expanded capacity.

Moving forward, Aramex flagged further impact on its profitability in the first half of 2025 from nearshoring volume flows from existing customers. While the group could not yet quantify the financial effect of US tariffs, it said the tariffs may impact its operations.

data.title: Pakistan Stocks Crash on Military Tensions
data.description:
The Pakistan stock Exchange's benchmark KSE 100 plunged toward the 103,000 mark on Thursday before trading was halted after strikes between Pakistan and India risked a war between both countries.

The decline marked a 16% plunge since hitting the record high of 120,000 last month.

Reports of explosions in Lahore one day after Indian air strikes in Pakistani territory, claimed to be terrorist infrastructure by New Delhi, drove Pakistan to vow retaliation and extend the conflict.

The risk of an escalated conflict coincided with signs that the Pakistan economy was on a road to recovery from its crisis, while the government attempts to shore up finances and progress on the $7 billion loan by the IMF last year.

Still, stocks in multiple sectors recovered from session loans ahead of the IMF's decision on a possible extension to the country's debt payment.

data.title: REG - Centrica PLC - Update on Share Buyback Extension
data.description:
RNS Number : 8778H Centrica PLC 08 May 2025  

Centrica plc

Update on Share Buyback Extension

In its 2024 Preliminary Results on 20 February 2025, the Company announced a further £500 million extension of the Company's share buyback programme, to be completed by around the end of 2025 (the "2025 Extension"), taking the total programme to £2.0bn.

The Company today announces the entry into an irrevocable arrangement with Goldman Sachs International ("Goldman Sachs") (acting as riskless principal) to conduct the repurchases in respect of the first £250 million tranche of the 2025 Extension (the "First Tranche") on its behalf and to make trading decisions in respect of the First Tranche independently of the Company.

Repurchases in respect of the First Tranche will be conducted from 16 June 2025 to no later than 19 September 2025 to buy back ordinary shares of 6 14/81 pence each (the "Shares") up to an aggregate maximum consideration of up to £250 million (exclusive of associated fees, expenses and stamp duty), representing an amount equal to the aggregate value of approximately 3% of the Company's issued share capital at the share price as at close of business on 7 May 2025.

Goldman Sachs International may effect purchases of shares under the First Tranche on the London Stock Exchange and/or other trading venues[1] for subsequent purchase by the Company. Purchases by the Company will be treated as being made on the London Stock Exchange. The Company intends that any shares purchased will be cancelled, held in treasury, or used for the purposes of employee share schemes.

The purpose of the 2025 Extension is to reduce the capital of the Company.

Any share purchases will be carried out in accordance with certain pre-set parameters. The maximum number of ordinary shares which may be purchased by the Company under the First Tranche is  536,039,506 (which is the maximum pursuant to the authority granted by shareholders at the Company's Annual General Meeting held on 5 June 2024) less the number of Shares purchased (and to be purchased) by the Company pursuant to the share buyback programmes announced on 8 August 2024 and 27 December 2024 taken together with such number of Shares under any subsequent authority to repurchase Shares as may be granted by the Company's shareholders at the Company's Annual General Meeting to be held on 8 May 2025.

The First Tranche will be conducted within the parameters prescribed by the Market Abuse Regulation 596/2014/EU (as in force in the UK and as amended by the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019), the Commission Delegated Regulation 2016/1052/EU (as in force in the UK and as amended by the FCA's Technical Standards (Market Abuse Regulation) (EU Exit) Instrument 2019) and in accordance with Chapter 9 of the UK Listing Rules. No repurchases will be made in respect of the Company's American Depositary Receipts.

The Company will make further announcements in due course following any repurchase of shares. There is no guarantee that the First Tranche will be implemented in full or that any shares will be bought back by the Company.

Enquiries:

Investors and Analysts

E: ir@centrica.com

Media

T: 01784 843000

E: media@centrica.com

END

Centrica plc is listed on the London Stock Exchange (CNA)

Legal Entity Identifier number: E26EDV109X6EEPBKVH76

data.title: Holiday-Inn owner IHG defies industry trend with US growth
data.description:
Key points:
  • IHG's Global RevPAR rises 3.3% in first quarter
  • IHG reports 3.5% growth in US room revenue
  • Expects to meet annual profit consensus
  • Shares rise 2.7%

Holiday Inn owner InterContinental Hotels Group said on Thursday it was on track to meet market expectations for annual profit after a rebound in U.S. room revenues.

The United States is IHG's largest market, which faces heightened recession risks due to a global trade war sparked by President Donald Trump's tariffs, prompting travel companies to caution about stalling demand.

The downturn in travel following a post-pandemic boom has spooked the industry, prompting IHG's peers Marriott and Hilton to cut their full-year guidance.

IHG, which owns Crowne Plaza and Six Senses, has been investing in international expansion and a rebound in its U.S. market to counter sluggish domestic demand in China.

It reported 3.5% growth in U.S. room revenue for the three months ended March 31, compared with a 1.9% fall last year.

Its global revenue per available room (RevPAR) rose 3.3% in the first quarter, up from last year's 2.6% growth, but slightly slower than the 4.1% growth it saw in the prior quarter.

IHG reported continued room revenue growth into the second quarter and is banking on demand for domestic stays across its markets though it noted some softening of forward economic indicators.

Shares in the company were up 2.7% at 8,840 pence at 0808 GMT.

Analysts expect IHG to report core earnings of $1.32 billion pounds for the fiscal year 2025 according to a company compiled poll.

In 2024, the company reported $1.19 billion in core earnings.

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0823 GMT - The Irish state should return AIB Group to private ownership within a few months, analyst John Cronin of Seapoint Insights says. The announcement of an off-market 1.2 billion euro buyback will cut the government's stake to 3.3%. The state could retrieve the full 20.8 billion euros it invested in the bank when it bailed it out, he notes. "There had been much speculation in the market as to whether this directed buyback would take place given that the share price has been trading below the level at which the buyback will be executed," Cronin says. The small 1% difference was minor, so shareholders likely welcome the state's swifter exit, which will lead to the normalization of returns. Shares rise 1.2% to 6.275 euros. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0811 GMT - United Overseas Bank's earnings should remain resilient this year, as around 85% of its loan book is exposed to Asean and Greater China, CGS International analysts write in a note. The Singapore bank could benefit from the rerouting of trade flows away from the U.S., they say. However, potentially steeper rate cuts by the Fed coupled with a lagged effect of loan repricing could exacerbate margin pressure in 2H, they add. UOB could also continue to recognize higher general provisions for the remainder of the year. CGS International cuts its target price for the stock to S$38.60 from S$38.80, while maintaining an add rating. Shares are down 0.2% at S$34.41. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0806 GMT - Caution is warranted for Irish banks given the uncertainties ahead, J.P. Morgan says in a research note after AIB Group and Bank of Ireland's trading updates. These uncertainties stem from U.S. policy risks and their potential long-term impact on the country's economy, which could lead to weaker sentiment and slower balance sheet momentum, analysts Sheel Shah and Kian Abouhossein write. They also point to risks from interest-rate cuts given that Irish banks are highly rate-sensitive. Because of these, the analysts prefer having exposure to U.K. banks. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0801 GMT - Metro Bank's loan book only edged up on quarter when adjusted for its recent disposal, which is disappointing when compared with consensus expectations and the bank's mid-term growth guidance, Keefe, Bruyette & Woods says in a note. Despite this softness, the group backed its guidance and noted that it was profitable on underlying and statutory bases for the three-month period. Achieving its ambitious plans for market-leading profitability is still demanding despite the support from the winding down of old loans, analysts Edward Firth and Elise Yu Ge write. This requires consistent double-digit new business volume growth alongside expanding margins and maintaining credit quality, they note. Shares rise 2.7% to 107 pence. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0801 GMT - Metro Bank shares rise to their highest price in five months after the high-street lender's first-quarter update, in which it confirmed it is on track to meet its guidance. Shares were up 3.6% to 108 pence, their highest price since late November. It was reassuring to learn that the quarter was profitable, analyst John Cronin of SeaPoint Insights says. This evidences that the asset rotation and deposit optimisation strategy is working, he says. The reduction in net loans encompasses a portfolio disposal and the run-off of the rest of consumer and government-backed lending portfolios, showing that there is some core net loan growth coming through too. The fall in deposits is also a good thing as this reflects funding-cost optimization, he adds. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0756 GMT - Adecco delivered an in-line set of first-quarter results overall, Jefferies analyst Andrew Brooke says in a research note. The recruitment company's earnings beat expectations, but only because of a one-time boost from its FESCO joint venture in China, Jefferies says. Adecco's organic growth was slightly better than expected but still negative, while margins were mostly in line. The stock is trading at the low end of its historical value range and could rise in the medium term, though uncertain economic conditions could weigh on its short-term performance, Brooke adds. Shares are up 2.4% at 21.22 Swiss francs.(maitane.sardon@wsj.com)

0755 GMT - Hong Kong property stocks could face positive sentiment thanks to strong visitor numbers during public holidays in May, CGS International analysts write in a note. Visitors from mainland China rose 20% on year while foreign visitors increased 31% compared with a year earlier, which has boosted local retail sales and hotel occupancy, they say. The brokerage expects retail sales in Hong Kong to stabilize this year after a 7% decline in fiscal year 2024. In addition, recent strong capital inflow to Hong Kong of around HK$130 billion over May 2-May 8 should bode well for Hong Kong and its property sector. Lower interest rates in Hong Kong could spur more home purchases as well, CGS International adds.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0749 GMT - Mondi's first-quarter update suggests the FTSE 100 paper-and-packaging company is well under way to meet or exceed market expectations for the first half, Stifel's Lars Kjellberg and Massimiliano Pilato say in a research note. The company's first-quarter underlying Ebitda was 13% ahead of Stifel's estimate, the analysts say. This included a strong performance in its corrugated and flexible packaging operations and weak conditions in uncoated fine paper, the analysts say. While Mondi provided no guidance as usual, its comments point to stronger prices in the second quarter against a backdrop of economic uncertainty, Stifel says. Shares are up 3.8% at 11.52 pounds. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0749 GMT - Oil prices tick higher in early trade supported by optimism over upcoming trade talks between Washington and Beijing. Brent crude rises 0.4% to $61.34 a barrel, while WTI is up 0.5% to $58.34 a barrel. Senior officials from the world's two largest crude consumers are set to meet in Switzerland this weekend for the first direct talks during President Trump's second term. Separately, the U.S. is expected to announce a framework of a trade deal with the U.K. later on Thursday. However, enthusiasm around trade developments was tempered by demand concerns after the Federal Reserve warned of rising economic risks. "This boosted the USD, which added to headwinds facing the broader commodity markets," analysts at ING say. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

0748 GMT - EDP Renovaveis's first-quarter results point to a significant improvement in the company's underlying performance, RBC Capital Markets analysts say in a research note. The Portuguese renewable energy company reported a 5% increase in first-quarter recurring Ebitda to 467 million euros despite including no gains from asset sales, RBC says. This was higher than consensus estimates, and Ebitda jumped 20% when excluding asset gains, RBC says. However, net debt at the end of the quarter was 8.92 billion euros and RBC had forecast it at 8.6 billion euros. Shares fall 0.7% to 8.03 euros. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0748 GMT - Stock markets rise on increased optimism about easing trade tensions after U.S. President Donald Trump said that he would announce a major trade deal later. People familiar with the negotiations said Trump would announce a framework of a trade deal with the U.K. "The news boosted sentiment across global equity markets, lifting Nasdaq and S&P 500 futures, while also sending FTSE futures and sterling higher in Asian trading," IG analysts say in a note. The pan-European index Stoxx Europe 600 climbs 0.2% to 534.95 while the U.K. FTSE 100 stocks index rises 0.1% to 8,564.7 points and U.S. stock futures point to a higher opening on Wall Street. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0745 GMT - The biggest surprise in Zurich Insurance's quarterly print was the 44% like-for-like growth in the present value of new business premiums of its largest life insurance business, Jefferies says in a research note. This business is in EMEA and compares with a 20% contraction in the smaller Asia-Pacific life unit, the analysts add. Against the rapid growth in life, the non-life business results--which are a larger contributor to profits and saw single digit percentage increases--seem to more closely reflect investors views, analyst Philip Kett writes. Shares slip 0.7% to 589 Swiss francs. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

data.title: Irish Continental Group's Revenue in Four-months to April Climbs
data.description:
Irish Continental Group (IR5B.IR, ) said Thursday its revenue in the four months ended April 30 increased year over year.

The Irish shipping and transport group's consolidated group revenue was 189.5 million euros, compared with 177 million euros previously.

data.title: Swisscom drops on 'weak' Q1 results
data.description:
** Swisscom falls 2.6% on what ING bank calls 'weak' Q1 results ahead of the Vodafone Italia integration

** The Swiss telecoms group's Q1 EBITDAaL was down 6.6% on a pro forma basis, while revenue was 1.2% lower

** ING notes weak domestic performance and profitability decline in highly competitive Italian telecom market

** The stock is at the bottom of the Swiss blue-chip index

data.title: Taiwan Trade Surplus Tops Forecasts
data.description:
Taiwan’s trade surplus widened to a three-month high of USD 7.21 billion in April 2025, from USD 6.27 billion in the same month of the previous year, exceeding market expectations of USD 6.38 billion.

Exports surged by 29.9% year-on-year to USD 48.66 billion in April, primarily due to higher sales across all major export commodities, namely; information, communication, and audio-video products (60.5%), parts of electronic product (26.8%), chemicals (15.2%), base metals and articles of base metal (7.6%), and machinery (7.1%).

Meanwhile, imports soared by 33% from a year ealrier to USD 41.46 billion, mainly driven by increased purchases of information, communication, and audio-video products (228.8%), parts of electronic product (31.6%), and machinery (30.1%).

Considering the first four months of the year, the country’s trade surplus was recorded at USD 30.85 billion, a 21.6% higher compared to the previous year, with exports growing 20.6% and imports increasing by 20.4%.

data.title: Packaging firm Mondi's profit rises sequentially, says tariff impact limited
data.description:
Packaging firm Mondi reported a sequential increase in first-quarter profit on Thursday, as cost cuts and fewer maintenance shutdowns helped offset the industry-wide subdued demand, and said the direct impact of tariffs was limited.

The group's shares were up about 3.3% at 1,146.5 pence.

Mondi is banking on price hikes to bolster it from this quarter and is also ramping up capacity for its sustainable packaging products.

Mondi CEO Andrew King cautioned of the direct and indirect impacts of rising trade tensions on an industry that has been trying to recover from a slowdown following a pandemic-driven boom.

"While the direct impact of announced tariffs on our operations is limited, we remain mindful of the potential second-order impacts that could affect trade flows, consumer confidence and supply chains," King said in a statement.

Last month, Packaging Corp of America forecast current-quarter profit below expectations as tariffs both hit demand and led to higher freight and logistics costs.

Mondi said its reported underlying earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortisation rose to 290 million euros ($327.2 million) in the first quarter from 261 million euros in the fourth quarter.

Jefferies analysts said Mondi looks well-positioned for strong earnings growth and an upside to free cash flow "when packaging ultimately recovers."

($1 = 0.8863 euros)

data.title: Norwegian Krone Remains Lower After Rates Held SteadyMarket Talk
data.description: The Norwegian krone remains on the back foot after the Norges Bank held its key interest rate at 4.5%, as widely expected, and reiterated that it expects to start cutting rates later this year. "There is uncertainty about future economic developments, but the Committee's current assessment of the outlook implies that the policy rate will most likely be reduced in the course of 2025," the central bank Deputy Governor Pal Longva said. Inflation has fallen markedly but remains above target, he said. If the policy rate is lowered prematurely, prices may continue to rise rapidly. The euro rises 0.1% to 11.7141 krone, compared to 11.7214 before the decision. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: UK PM Starmer: You will hear more from me later today on US economic deal
data.description:
• You will hear more from me later today on US economic deal.
  • I will always act in national interest.

data.title: Anheuser-Busch InBev Q1 Adjusted Earnings Increase, Revenue Declines
data.description:
Anheuser-Busch InBev reported Q1 adjusted earnings Thursday of $0.81 per share, up from $0.75 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected $0.74.

Revenue for the quarter was $13.63 billion, down from $14.55 billion a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $13.80 billion.

Shares of the company were 2.6% higher in premarket activity.

data.title: Foxconn Secures 100% Stake in Q-Edge Corp.
data.description:
Hon Hai Precision Industry's , d/b/a Foxconn's, Foxconn Assembly Holding purchased 3.6 million shares in Q-Edge Corp. for $36 million, or $10 per share.

The Taiwanese semiconductor manufacturer's unit now owns all 3,820,000 shares in Q-Edge, according to a Thursday filing.

data.title: Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0748 GMT - EDP Renovaveis's first-quarter results point to a significant improvement in the company's underlying performance, RBC Capital Markets analysts say in a research note. The Portuguese renewable energy company reported a 5% increase in first-quarter recurring Ebitda to 467 million euros despite including no gains from asset sales, RBC says. This was higher than consensus estimates, and Ebitda jumped 20% when excluding asset gains, RBC says. However, net debt at the end of the quarter was 8.92 billion euros and RBC had forecast it at 8.6 billion euros. Shares fall 0.7% to 8.03 euros. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0655 GMT - A consensus downgrade for Centrica's earnings per share is expected despite the company confirming its adjusted operating profit guidance, JPMorgan analysts say in a research note. The analysts expect a decrease in the metric by 5% to 8% as a result of the update, they say. "We would expect the shares to underperform the market today especially given the strong performance year-to-date," they say. However, the analysts see upside to the shares of the U.K. energy company, which owns British Gas, in the medium term. (nina.kienle@wsj.com)

0048 GMT - What Infratil decides to do with a 10% stake in Contact Energy will be interesting, says Forsyth Barr. Infratil will have the equity interest, currently worth around NZ$900 million, once Contact's takeover of Manawa Energy completes. Analyst Aaron Ibbotson doesn't see it as a long-term holding for Infratil. "Infratil has two more mature assets in the NZ$1 billion size bracket--Wellington International Airport and Diagnostic Imaging," Forsyth Barr. "Both assets are too small to matter to Infratil now but too big to ignore." Forsyth Barr expects Infratil to consider strategic options for both. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

0044 GMT - Contact Energy will have plenty of options once its takeover of Manawa Energy completes, but its balance sheet may need some help, suggests Forsyth Barr. Contact's combination with Manawa creates New Zealand's second-largest power generator, and analyst Andrew Harvey-Green says the size of its development pipeline also ranks second in the sector. "However, with Contact taking on additional debt, its net debt/Ebitdaf will be well above 3x in FY 2026 and FY 2027, meaning funding more developments on balance sheet will be difficult without an equity raise," Forsyth Barr says.(david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

2209 GMT - Uncertainties about the Trump administration's clean-energy policies are not slowing down the industry's expansion outside the U.S., Jim Coulter, executive chairman of TPG, says during an earnings call. "They seem unbothered by the discussion that is going on about [the] U. S. policy," Coulter says of overseas markets. He points to the more than $2 trillion the world spent in clean-energy development last year, double the spending across fossil-fuel sectors. He also mentions the recently agreed acquisition by a TPG-led group of investors of a 90% stake in Siemens Gamesa's offshore wind business in India. "Around the world, north of 90% of the energy addition was happening through clean energy," he says. "[Those] markets are continuing apace and if anything, accelerating." (luis.garcia@wsj.com; @lhvgarcia)

1851 GMT - Crude futures retreat after yesterday's technical rebound as concerns about rising OPEC+ production outweigh positive signs of U.S.-China trade talks and a second weekly draw in U.S. crude stocks. Most of the moving parts in the EIA inventory data were on the bearish side, except for the 98,000 barrels-a-day drop in U.S. production, Mizuho's Robert Yawger says ina note, pointing to higher imports, lower exports, and virtually no change in inputs to refineries. Oil prices slipped a bit further after the Fed left interest rates unchanged and said uncertainty about the economic outlook has increased. WTI settles down 1.7% at $58.07 a barrel, and Brent falls 1.7% to $61.12. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1627 GMT - Crude futures turn lower after a steady start, returning some of the previous day's gains as OPEC+ production plans keep buyers cautious. A tentative start to U.S.-China talks this week is seen supportive of oil, as are comments from U.S. producers about curtailing drilling because of low prices. The EIA reported a 2 million barrel decline in crude oil stockpiles for last week, while gasoline stocks rose for the first time in more than two months. WTI and Brent are down 1.3% at $58.31 and $61.34 a barrel, respectively. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1331 GMT - Oil futures are holding their ground after yesterday's rebound, supported by plans for trade talks this week between U.S. and Chinese officials and an industry report of a large U.S. crude stock draw. "The energy complex appears to be pricing in less tariff-related demand deterioration than previously expected," Ritterbusch says in a note. The API's report of a 4.5 million barrel crude inventory draw for last week, "especially given the 0.9 million barrel reduction in Cushing supply," is supportive of crude, the firm adds. Closely watched EIA inventory data are due at 10:30 a.m. ET. WTI is trading flat at $59.09 a barrel, and Brent is off 0.1% at $62.11 a barrel. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

0946 GMT - Orsted's decision to discontinue development of the Hornsea 4 offshore wind farm in the U.K. is an eye opener, Barclays analysts say in a note. The renewable-energy company continues to struggle, they say. Orsted will be hurt by the worsening news surrounding offshore wind in the U.S., uncertainty over tariffs for U.S. onshore wind, and the saturation of renewables in European markets. A turnaround would require clarity on U.S. tariffs and individual retirement savings plans, and evidence of investor appetite for secondary asset sales, Barclays says. The proceeds from these sales could be reinvested into higher-yielding areas. The added benefit of this would be reduced capex if assets are sold earlier in the construction cycle, Barclays adds.(anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

data.title: Basic Materials Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Basic Materials. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0749 GMT - Mondi's first-quarter update suggests the FTSE 100 paper-and-packaging company is well under way to meet or exceed market expectations for the first half, Stifel's Lars Kjellberg and Massimiliano Pilato say in a research note. The company's first-quarter underlying Ebitda was 13% ahead of Stifel's estimate, the analysts say. This included a strong performance in its corrugated and flexible packaging operations and weak conditions in uncoated fine paper, the analysts say. While Mondi provided no guidance as usual, its comments point to stronger prices in the second quarter against a backdrop of economic uncertainty, Stifel says. Shares are up 3.8% at 11.52 pounds. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0657 GMT - Solvay's 1Q performance could result in share-price pressure as it now forecasts to reach the lower end of its earnings guidance range and expects 2Q earnings to come in significantly lower, Jefferies analysts say in a note. The Belgium chemical company's adjusted Ebitda was in line with consensus but this included a one-off gain of 10 million euros, the analysts say. Its sales missed market and Jefferies consensus, they add.(helena.smolak@wsj.com)

0335 GMT - Orica's 1H earnings result is strong, underpinned by demand for high-margin blasting products that offset a fall in ammonium nitrate volumes, UBS analysts say. In a note, they describe Orica's cash flow conversion as solid and say it has the balance sheet capacity to support its A$400 million share buyback. First-half earnings per share is 10% ahead of consensus, they say. UBS has a buy rating and A$22.00/share target on Orica, which is up 8.2% at A$18.15/share. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

data.description:

  • ATRAM GROUP & UNIONBANK FORMALIZE PARTNERSHIP TO ENHANCE FINANCIAL SERVICES

data.title: Styrene monomer (SM) prices plunge in Europe
data.description: On Wednesday, styrene monomer prices drifted sharply lower in the European region.An industry source in Europe, on condition of anonymity, informed a Polymerupdate team member,
